$comment: SPDX-License-Identifier: FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0. Copyright (c) 2025 Open Computer Use Contributors. Exec/PTY WebSocket control-frame envelope (Control API -> Session sandbox). One WebSocket per session; host-dialled, non-host peers rejected at accept (NFR-IC-03, NFR-SEC-43). Text frames carry the tagged-JSON control messages below; raw stdio travels as separate binary frames announced by the StdIo* control tags (raw bytes are not JSON, so they are described structurally here, not validated). Per RFC 6455 WebSocket; control tags are an OCU-defined union.
title: ExecChannelMessage
description: A single tagged-JSON control frame on the exec WebSocket. The discriminator is the sole top-level property name (an externally-tagged union). Binary stdio frames are out of band of this schema and follow a StdIo* announce frame.

Signal.$comment: A signal is either a number (1..64) or a POSIX name; type/length/pattern bounded to strict-validate input (NFR-SEC-51). The discriminator is the message key, not this value's type.

TraceEvent.$comment: A trace event, Chrome-Trace-Event-shaped. The body is pinned to exactly these five fields and closed (additionalProperties:false): name and ph are required; cat, ts, and dur_us are optional. ONE shared definition referenced by both the server and client TraceEvent tags, so the two directions can never drift. The five field names (name/cat/ph/ts/dur_us) and the Chrome-trace shape are an evidence-led contract choice, pinned here as the trace contract. ph is a single ASCII phase letter. ts is microseconds since an unspecified epoch; like memory_limit_bytes the integer carries a u64 wire-type domain, so no JSON-number maximum is expressed (the ceiling is a wire-type bound, not a numeric maximum a validator would round).

ts.$comment: microseconds since an unspecified epoch; the u64 ceiling is a wire-type bound, NOT a JSON-number maximum (a validator would round 2^64-1 out of IEEE-754 safe-integer range).

BoundedReason.$comment: NFR-SEC-51: bounded error verbosity. Caller gets a stable reason code; message must not carry stack traces or internal topology. Length capped.

message.$comment: x-ocu-default: maxLength 256 is an NFR-SEC-51 default pending sign-off, not a source-pinned value; retunable per workload tier.

CreateProcess.$comment: Spawn parameters. env-scrub and uid/gid drop are the no-creds-in-guest carriers; the schema records the fields, the sandbox enforces the scrub. The cmd/cwd/args-item maxLength 4096, args maxItems 1024, and env maxProperties 1024 are x-ocu-default bounds satisfying NFR-SEC-46 (a bound must exist) pending sign-off on the value; retunable per workload tier. The env-value maxLength 32768 matches the negotiated WebSocket buffer ceiling (NFR-SEC-46). Integer maxima are wire-type ceilings (uint8/16/32/64), not policy.

memory_limit_bytes.$comment: per-process cap (u64 bytes); breach -> ProcessOutOfMemory. The u64 ceiling (2^64-1) is not expressed as a JSON-number maximum — it exceeds IEEE-754 safe-integer range and a validator would round it; the 64-bit domain is enforced by the wire type.

ProcessConnection.$comment: Handshake envelope (client message 1 or 2). create_req present => spawn; absent => reattach. expected_container_name and the out-of-band JWT sub both carry NFR-SEC-43 container binding. want_trace_events and accept_compression are the client side of capability negotiation: the client advertises them in the handshake; the server answers with the ConnectionCapabilities first frame. The compression flag's pinned algorithm (one self-contained zstd frame per binary stdio frame) is described on accept_compression.

accept_compression.$comment: Client advertises it accepts frame compression. The algorithm is pinned: each compressed binary frame is ONE self-contained zstd frame (RFC 8878) with a window no larger than 2^17 bytes; only the raw stdio binary frames are compressed (the tagged-JSON text control frames are never compressed), gated on the negotiated supports_compression. The flag itself stays type:boolean — pinning the algorithm changes no wire shape.

ConnectionCapabilities.$comment: First server frame after the handshake. Per-feature negotiation, not a version integer: each flag toggles one capability so the protocol evolves without a global version bump. The V1/V2 split is expressed through the message tags (ProcessCreated vs ProcessCreatedV2, AttachedToProcess vs AttachedToProcessV2), not a version field in the handshake. supports_compression advertises that frames may be compressed; the algorithm is pinned (one self-contained zstd frame per binary stdio frame, RFC 8878), described on supports_compression.

supports_compression.$comment: Server advertises frame compression support. The algorithm is pinned: each compressed binary frame is ONE self-contained zstd frame (RFC 8878) with a window no larger than 2^17 bytes; only the raw stdio binary frames are compressed (the tagged-JSON text control frames are never compressed), gated on this negotiated flag. The flag itself stays type:boolean — pinning the algorithm changes no wire shape.

ProcessCreatedV2.$comment: V2 spawn-success variant. The body is deliberately empty until the V2 status carrier is pinned: additionalProperties:false with no properties validates exactly {} and rejects any keyed object. Both V2 tags are kept (a symmetric wire pair). Pinning the status set later is a forward-compatible revision that ADDS properties, not a re-opening (see x-ocu-open-questions).

AttachedToProcessV2.$comment: V2 reattach-success variant. The body is deliberately empty until the V2 status carrier is pinned: additionalProperties:false with no properties validates exactly {} and rejects any keyed object — same deliberately-empty closure as ProcessCreatedV2.

ProcessAlreadyAttached.$comment: NFR-IC-05: single owner per pid (sequential-default concurrency).

FailedToStart.$comment: Generic start failure. Distinct from FailedToStartProcessWithSameIdRunning per OCU-canon split.

ExpectStdOut.$comment: Announce frame: the next binary frame on the socket is stdout, bounded by max-ws-buffer-size 32768 (NFR-SEC-46).

ExpectStdErr.$comment: Announce frame: next binary frame is stderr, bounded by NFR-SEC-46.

TraceEvent.$comment: Server-side trace event; emitted only when supports_traces was negotiated. The body is the pinned, closed shared TraceEvent shape — the SAME $defs/TraceEvent the client tag references, so the two directions never drift.

ProcessExited.$comment: Normal exit, carrying the exit code only. Signal-terminated and signal-stopped are distinct internal termination reasons but have no distinct wire variant in any source; they surface through this same code field (a 128+signal convention is the sandbox's choice, not asserted here). No source exposes code/signal as separate serialized fields.

ExpectStdIn.$comment: OCU-canon: announce-only. The next binary frame carries stdin bytes (bounded by max-ws-buffer-size 32768, NFR-SEC-46); a non-binary follow-up is an error.

StdInEOF.$comment: OCU-canon: the client has no further stdin; the guest closes the child's stdin write side. Symmetric to the server-side StdOutEOF/StdErrEOF announces. An explicit tag is required because a zero-length binary frame is indistinguishable from an empty write.

Detach.$comment: Leaves the process running; valid only when create_req.reattachable was true.

TraceEvent.$comment: Client-side trace event; valid only when supports_traces was negotiated. The body is the pinned, closed shared TraceEvent shape — the SAME $defs/TraceEvent the server tag references, so the two directions never drift.

x-ocu-open-questions: ProcessCreatedV2 / AttachedToProcessV2 payload body: both V2 success tags are now closed-empty (additionalProperties:false, no properties — they validate exactly {}), which is the only V2 body emitted today. The expected future payload is a server-tracked status set (memory_usage_bytes, memory_cgroup_path, process_group_pid, internal_state with states CgroupCreated/Ready), but its carrier frame is unconfirmed. Pinning that status set is a FUTURE forward-compatible revision that ADDS properties to these empty bodies — not a re-opening; the tags stay closed until that revision lands.
$comment-anyOf: A frame is the phase-1 handshake (ProcessConnection, identified by its required process_id) or a post-handshake control frame. Frame direction — server-sent vs client-sent — is a transport property (which peer wrote it), not an envelope distinction, so the same tag may appear in both directions (e.g. TraceEvent flows both ways under a negotiated supports_traces). anyOf, not oneOf: a TraceEvent frame is a valid control frame in either direction, and the envelope does not assert mutual exclusivity the wire does not have.
